title: Willis Sustainable Fuels Names Wilton International as Site for Its Sustainable Aviation Fuel Project
content: Willis Sustainable Fuels (UK) Limited, a subsidiary of Willis Lease Finance Corporation, has selected Wilton International in Teesside as the site for its new Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) project. The facility will be built in partnership with Sembcorp Utilities (UK) Limited and aims to produce 14,000 tonnes of SAF annually from 2028, cutting over 80% of greenhouse gas emissions compared to conventional jet fuel. Backed by a Â£2.9 million grant from the UK Department for Transportâ€™s Advanced Fuels Fund, the plant will convert sustainable biomethane from waste into aviation fuel. Technology collaborators include Johnson Matthey and Axens, with McDermott leading engineering efforts. The initiative will create about 50 permanent highly-skilled jobs and 200 construction jobs, supporting Teessideâ€™s role in the UKâ€™s energy transition. Sembcorp Energy UK CEO Mike Patrick expressed the projectâ€™s importance in accelerating the regionâ€™s net-zero ambitions. The announcement includes forward-looking statements reflecting future expectations and possible risks, as outlined in regulatory disclosures.
author: Analysts Account
